description: Dense jottings in Judaeo-Arabic and Arabic. All of these appear to be a mixture of religiously and erotically inflected panegyric, addressing "the king of the world, the caliph of creation, Righteous son of Perfect," etc., but presumably not the actual caliph, as the references to wine and union would seem out of line. Then again, ʿĀdil b. Kāmil could be a reference to al-ʿĀdil II (regent 1232–38, reigned 1238–40). There are also some (nonsense?) strings of similar-sounding words.
description_he: רישומים צפופים בערבית-יהודית וערבית. כולם נראים תערובת של פאנגיריק בעל אופי דתי ואירוטי, הפונה אל 'מלך עולם, ח'ליפה הבריאה, בן צדיק של שלם' וכיוצא בזה.
